title: Hurricane Michael (Bay Panhandle wind benchmark)
benchmark_focus: Panhandle wind benchmark focused on extreme wind, roof failure, and scope-of-repair disputes.
key_facts: The insured location experienced catastrophic wind conditions with widespread roof covering and structural envelope damage.
Post-loss inspections documented displaced roofing, broken openings, and rain intrusion tied to wind-created openings.
The carrier position limited covered damage to spot repairs and challenged the need for full roof replacement.
notes: Use this scenario for Panhandle wind cases where the factual dispute is less about surge and more about severe wind forces and repair scope.
Carrier is intentionally a repeatable default homeowners carrier rather than a claim-specific benchmark dependency.
